+  scope guard can parse it as an explicit code token. Example of a valid

+  (for example ``- Invoke-AiDispatchLoop.ps1`` with no backticks) are invalid
+  for the queue scope guard and must not appear in the generated TASK packet.

Required change: in `Invoke-AiDispatchLoop.ps1`, update only the existing `Invoke-PlanFill` planner prompt Rules list so the source correctly escapes literal PowerShell backticks around `Invoke-AiDispatchLoop.ps1`. The source path token should be bracketed by doubled PowerShell backticks so the runtime prompt evaluates to the single-backtick Markdown form shown in Updated Acceptance Criteria. Do not change runtime logic, parsers, templates, schemas, docs, or queue behavior. Acceptance: evaluating the added prompt snippet through PowerShell here-string semantics shows the valid example line with `Invoke-AiDispatchLoop.ps1` wrapped in Markdown backticks.
+

+None. The control review identified only the prompt-example escaping defect above.

+
+Unchanged from Task Packet, with this explicit clarification: the valid worked example in the runtime planner prompt MUST contain this exact Markdown backtick-wrapped path token:

+```text

+
+## Halt Conditions (Updated if Any)

+
+Keep the correction narrowly scoped to the existing `Invoke-PlanFill` planner prompt Rules list. The canonical verification log already passed before this semantic review failure, so the only required source change is the PowerShell escaping needed to preserve literal Markdown backticks in the runtime prompt.
+

+# Execution Report
+
+DISPATCH_ID: ISSUE-202
+AUTHOR: Executor / Claude
+TIMESTAMP: 2026-05-26_08-26-06+0300
+RELATED_FILES:
+- Invoke-AiDispatchLoop.ps1 — fixed PowerShell escaping on the worked-example line in the `Invoke-PlanFill` planner prompt `Rules:` list so the runtime prompt evaluates to the Markdown backtick-wrapped path token required by the Correction Packet
+- ai_handoffs/ISSUE-202_CORRECT_2026-05-26_08-19-29+0300.md — correction packet consumed (reference only, unedited)
+- ai_handoffs/ISSUE-202_EXEC_2026-05-26_08-26-06+0300.md — this execution report
+STATUS: AWAITING_REVIEW
+
+## Task Packet Reference
+
+`ai_handoffs/ISSUE-202_TASK_2026-05-26_08-06-23+0300.md`
+
+(See also Correction Packet: `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-202_CORRECT_2026-05-26_08-19-29+0300.md` —
+the source of this execution round's single approved change.)
+
+## Pre-Execution Review Consumed
+
+This is a CORRECTION round. The Codex control review for round 0 was
+recorded as the gitignored run-dir JSON
+`.ai/dispatch-<DispatchId>/codex.control.round0.json` (no `_REVIEW_` packet
+is emitted for orchestrator dispatches per AGENTS.md). The Planner
+distilled that review into the Correction Packet above; the Executor
+concurs with the single approved correction (the worked-example backtick
+escaping defect) and acted on exactly that scope.
+
+## What I Changed
+
+### Source
+
+- `Invoke-AiDispatchLoop.ps1` (line 952, inside the `Invoke-PlanFill`
+  planner prompt double-quoted here-string): changed the inner backticks
+  wrapping the path token in the worked example from single PowerShell
+  backticks to doubled PowerShell backticks. Specifically:
+  - Before: `` bullet: ``- `Invoke-AiDispatchLoop.ps1` ``. ``
+  - After:  `` bullet: ``- ``Invoke-AiDispatchLoop.ps1`` ``. ``
+  In a PowerShell double-quoted here-string, single `` ` `` adjacent to a
+  letter or space is consumed as an escape (so the broken form rendered
+  as ` `- Invoke-AiDispatchLoop.ps1 ` ` — path NOT backtick-wrapped). The
+  doubled `` `` `` pair emits a literal `` ` `` character, so the runtime
+  prompt now contains the substring `- ` `Invoke-AiDispatchLoop.ps1` `
+  required by the Updated Acceptance Criteria.
+
+No runtime logic, parsers, validators, queue behavior, templates, schemas,
+docs, scheduler code, or task-brief content was modified. Only this one
+character-level escaping fix inside the existing planner prompt literal.
+
+### Tests
+
+- None added. The Correction Packet retained the Task Packet's "no tests
+  required" condition; satisfaction comes from parser validation, diff
+  inspection, snippet evaluation, and the canonical verification gate.
+
+### Docs
+
+- None added or modified. Docs are in the MUST NOT edit envelope for this
+  dispatch.
+
+## Per-File Summary
+
+- `Invoke-AiDispatchLoop.ps1` — Single-line PowerShell escaping fix inside
+  the `Invoke-PlanFill` planner prompt `Rules:` list (the worked-example
+  bullet on line 952). The `Rules:` block additions from the prior
+  execution round (lines 949–955) are otherwise unchanged. Here-string
+  delimiters `@"` / `"@`, the `$prompt =` assignment, the
+  `Invoke-CodexPrompt` call, and `Test-PacketFinalizeDryRun` are
+  untouched.
+
+## Verification Results
+
+- `git diff --check` → exit 0 (no whitespace/conflict markers).
+- PowerShell parser validation for `Invoke-AiDispatchLoop.ps1`
+  (`[System.Management.Automation.Language.Parser]::ParseFile(...)`) →
+  exit 0, output `PARSE_OK`, no parse errors.
+- PowerShell evaluation of the added `Invoke-PlanFill` prompt snippet
+  (the four-line `Rules:` example block, expanded through here-string
+  semantics) → exit 0, output `CONTAINS_TARGET_TOKEN: YES`. Rendered
+  example line:
+
+      Example of a valid bullet: `- `Invoke-AiDispatchLoop.ps1` `.
+
+  This contains the exact substring `- ` `Invoke-AiDispatchLoop.ps1` `
+  specified in the Correction Packet's Updated Acceptance Criteria.
+- `.\.ai\dispatch.verify.ps1` → exit 0
+  (`VERIFY OK: all 7 verification step(s) passed.`):
+  - [1] `cargo +nightly fmt --check` → ok (1s)
+  - [2] architecture lints (`cargo run -q -p rge-tool-architecture-lints -- all`) → ok (7s)
+  - [3] architecture-lint test suite (`cargo test -p rge-tool-architecture-lints --all-targets`) → ok
+  - [4]–[6] cargo build/test/doc-test gates → ok
+  - [7] `cargo bench -p rge-script-bench --no-run` → ok (0s)
+- Static prompt-only inspection (`git diff -- Invoke-AiDispatchLoop.ps1`):
+  the only source hunk vs `HEAD` is the prior round's seven-line `Rules:`
+  block insertion (with this round's one-character corrected escaping
+  embedded on the example line). The hunk lives entirely inside the
+  `Invoke-PlanFill` planner prompt literal between the

+# Task Packet
+
+DISPATCH_ID: ISSUE-202
+AUTHOR: Planner / OpenAI Codex
+TIMESTAMP: 2026-05-26_08-06-23+0300
+RELATED_FILES:
+- Invoke-AiDispatchLoop.ps1
+- ai_handoffs/ISSUE-202_TASK_2026-05-26_08-06-23+0300.md
+- ai_handoffs/ISSUE-202_EXEC_*.md
+- ai_dispatch_logs/log_*.md
+STATUS: OPEN
+
+## Goal
+
+Harden the Codex planner prompt grammar in `Invoke-AiDispatchLoop.ps1` so future generated TASK packets express queue-scope path and glob tokens in a form the scope guard can parse reliably. The implementation must be limited to the literal `Invoke-PlanFill` planner prompt `Rules:` list and must not alter dispatch runtime behavior.
+
+## Scope
+
+### MAY edit
+- `Invoke-AiDispatchLoop.ps1`
+- `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-202_*.md`
+- `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-202_*.meta.json`
+- `ai_dispatch_logs/log_*.md`
+
+### MUST NOT edit
+- `Invoke-AiDispatchQueue.ps1`
+- `Invoke-AiDispatchAuto.ps1`
+- `Register-AiDispatchSchedule.ps1`
+- `Get-AiDispatchHealth.ps1`
+- `Watch-AiDispatch.ps1`
+- `new-handoff.ps1`
+- `.ai/dispatch.tasks.md`
+- `.ai/*.schema.json`
+- `.ai/dispatch.verify.ps1`
+- `ai_handoffs/AI_HANDOFF_PROTOCOL.md`
+- `ai_handoffs/templates/*`
+- `AI_DISPATCH_AUTOMATION.md`
+- `AI_DISPATCH_PARALLEL.md`
+- `.github/workflows/*`
+- `Cargo.toml`
+- `Cargo.lock`
+- `crates/**`
+- `editor/**`
+- `kernel/**`
+- `runtime/**`
+- `*.rs`
+- Any scope guard parser implementation, templates, schemas, docs, Rust code, Cargo metadata, workflow files, scheduler code, or task brief content.
+
+### MAY add new files
+- `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-202_EXEC_*.md`
+- `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-202_EXEC_*.meta.json`
+- `ai_dispatch_logs/log_*.md`
+
+### MUST NOT add new files
+- Source files, tests, docs, schemas, templates, workflows, Cargo files, scheduler files, scope guard parser files, or task brief files.
+- Any non-artifact file outside this dispatch's own `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-202_*` and queue-run `ai_dispatch_logs/log_*.md` artifacts.
+
+## Deliverables
+
+- Update only the `Rules:` list inside the literal planner prompt string built by `Invoke-PlanFill` in `Invoke-AiDispatchLoop.ps1`.
+- Add planner-prompt wording requiring every path or glob token listed in generated TASK packets under `### MAY edit` and `### MAY add new files` to be wrapped in Markdown backticks.
+- Add planner-prompt wording stating that bare-bulleted paths or globs in those two sections are invalid for the queue scope guard.
+- Include a tiny worked example of an allowed backtick-quoted path token, for example ``- `Invoke-AiDispatchLoop.ps1` ``.
+- Do not change any runtime logic, parser behavior, validation behavior, queue behavior, templates, schemas, docs, or task-selection brief content.
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+
+- `Invoke-AiDispatchLoop.ps1` has a prompt-string-only diff: the only changed source lines are inside the `Invoke-PlanFill` planner prompt literal, within its `Rules:` list.
+- The revised planner prompt explicitly requires backtick wrapping for every path or glob token in `### MAY edit` and `### MAY add new files`.
+- The revised planner prompt explicitly says bare-bulleted paths or globs in those sections are invalid for the queue scope guard.
+- The revised planner prompt includes a tiny valid example such as ``- `Invoke-AiDispatchLoop.ps1` ``.
+- No behavior changes occur outside the literal prompt text.
+- No forbidden files are modified or added.
+
+## Constraints / Non-Goals
+
+- This is not a queue scope guard parser change.
+- This is not a template, schema, docs, Rust, Cargo, workflow, scheduler, or task brief change.
+- Do not update `Invoke-AiDispatchQueue.ps1` or `Invoke-AiDispatchAuto.ps1`.
+- Do not modify the generated packet parser, scope guard parser, or any queue runtime validation.
+- Do not broaden the planner prompt beyond the path-token grammar rule in the existing `Rules:` list.
+- Do not add tests unless the existing repository verification gate requires generated artifacts; this dispatch is satisfied by parser validation, diff inspection, and canonical verification.
+
+## Verification Gates
+
+The Executor MUST run and document the result of each of these in their `EXECUTION_REPORT`:
+
+- PowerShell parser validation for `Invoke-AiDispatchLoop.ps1`:
+  `powershell -NoProfile -Command '$tokens=$null; $errors=$null; [System.Management.Automation.Language.Parser]::ParseFile((Resolve-Path -LiteralPath "Invoke-AiDispatchLoop.ps1"), [ref]$tokens, [ref]$errors) | Out-Null; if ($errors.Count) { $errors | Format-List; exit 1 }'`
+- `git diff --check` -> expected exit 0.
+- `.\.ai\dispatch.verify.ps1` -> expected exit 0.
+- Static prompt-only inspection: run `git diff -- Invoke-AiDispatchLoop.ps1` and document that the only source hunk is inside the `Invoke-PlanFill` planner prompt literal `Rules:` list.
+- Scope inspection: run `git diff --name-only` and document that changed paths are limited to `Invoke-AiDispatchLoop.ps1`, `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-202_*`, and this dispatch's queue-run `ai_dispatch_logs/log_*.md` artifact.
+
+## Halt Conditions
+
+The Executor MUST halt without committing and request fresh guidance if any of the following occur:
+
+- The required wording cannot be placed solely in the `Invoke-PlanFill` planner prompt `Rules:` list.
+- Satisfying the issue appears to require editing `Invoke-AiDispatchQueue.ps1`, `Invoke-AiDispatchAuto.ps1`, any scope guard parser, templates, schemas, docs, Rust code, Cargo metadata, workflows, scheduler code, or `.ai/dispatch.tasks.md`.
+- Any implementation change would alter dispatch runtime behavior outside the literal planner prompt string.
+- Any verification gate fails after the prompt-only change.
+- Any untracked or modified non-artifact file outside the MAY edit/MAY add envelope appears during the work.
+
+## Planner Notes
+
+The point of this dispatch is to improve future planner output so the queue scope guard sees paths and globs as explicit Markdown code tokens. Keep the implementation surgical: add rules to the planner prompt text only. A valid example should show the whole bullet token, such as ``- `Invoke-AiDispatchLoop.ps1` ``, while a bare bullet such as `- Invoke-AiDispatchLoop.ps1` should be described as invalid for queue scope guard parsing.
+
